notes: Dante's loop on the riverboat. The verbs are how he runs the empire: watch from the helm window, pour bourbon neat (ice is for the weak), make the rounds among the tables, press the intercom to summon Sammy, maintain the brass railing on principle, set the card on the desk square to the corner. AUTHORITY (engine var _authority) is the spendable currency — the office's reservoir. LEDGER (engine var _ledger) is the positive doom-clock — THE LEDGER CLOSED. Doubt and stagnation are negative tracks.

cards.title: WATCH FROM THE HELM
cards.flavor: Stand at the leaded window the architect insisted on. The dining room sounds like a dining room from this angle. You can tell within a guest or two how the floor is doing without going down.

cards.title: POUR NEAT
cards.flavor: From the bottle in the file cabinet's second drawer. Neat, no ice. Ice is for the weak. Ice dilutes the truth of the thing. The amber liquid burns. The burn does little to warm the chill that has settled in your marrow years ago.

cards.title: MAKE THE ROUNDS
cards.flavor: Down the iron stair. Among the tables. Shake a hand. Bend to murmur something into Mrs. Karras's ear that makes her laugh, which has been the goal. Clap Sammy on the shoulder with affection that is not, for once, performative. Nod to Nicola.

cards.title: PRESS THE INTERCOM
cards.flavor: Press the brass button. The line connects to Sammy at the bar. Sammy. Yeah, Mr. D. Bring it up. Yes sir. Release the button. The intercom clicks. The room is the room.

cards.title: SET IT SQUARE TO THE CORNER
cards.flavor: Take the evidence in your hand. The card, the note, the page. Set it on the corner of the desk square. A man who burned his evidence was a man who was afraid. A man who kept his evidence square was a man who had decided to know.

cards.title: MAINTAIN THE BRASS
cards.flavor: Run a hand over the brass railing you installed in the second year. The bright brass is the wrong brass; you have known this since the first time you ran a hand over it; you have maintained it anyway on the principle that a man who maintains the wrong thing maintains something.

cards.title: MURMUR TO MRS. KARRAS
cards.flavor: Bend to her ear. Say the thing you have, over the years, learned to say to her — the small in-joke about her late husband's wedding ring and the time he had it re-sized in '83. She laughs. The laugh is, by year four, also a regular's loyalty.

cards.title: CALL · 1st SUNDAY · EVEN MONTH
cards.flavor: First Sunday of an even-numbered month. Dial Antonio. He picks up on the third ring. He always does. You both say the things you say. Neither of you says anything important. Both know this.

cards.title: OPEN THE CATERING OFFICE
cards.flavor: The lower-deck office. The desk lamp on. The phone with eleven messages. The October wedding in Port Arthur. The bride's father is somebody you did business with in '88. You sign the contract on the second page where the second page asks you to.

cards.title: THE BACK ROOM TAKE
cards.flavor: Step into the card room. The regulars do not, this hour, look up. You do not, this hour, want them to. The Saturday take is in the small leather satchel by the felt's edge. You take it. You leave. The room does not register your having been in it.

cards.title: THE DECREE
cards.flavor: Set down the ruling that the room remembers. Whoever is at your space yields their patience one turn longer. The brass railing brightens slightly. Once per night.
